{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"api_error_status":null,"duration_ms":371876,"duration_api_ms":372355,"ttft_ms":4033,"num_turns":45,"result":"Review complete. **Verdict: PASS_WITH_FINDINGS** (non-blocking).

CP00-451 is a sound descriptor-only pack. The pack-level posture is honest and well-supported: it declares `production_ready: false` / `implemented_pending_review`, defers enterprise trust to human approval, keeps Claude as a read-only reviewer (not final approver), and uses Hermes only for deterministic review-packet/receipt descriptors — never product runtime. Scope, exports, contract projection, validators, and tests are all internally consistent and the authority boundary is enforced in code, not merely asserted.

- **0 P0, 0 P1, 0 P2** — no blockers.
- **1 P3** — a cosmetic field-naming overlap in the generated `changed-file-scope.json` (the pack's own evidence directory is also listed under `unrelated_dirty_files_preserved`). No required file is omitted, so pack integrity is unaffected; safe to close out as-is.

All seven required public exports exist (service.js + validators.js) and re-export through the index.js barrel. The descriptor composes upstream CP450 output and is frozen with a complete no-write / no-real-data flag set (freezeCp451Result), and the validators actively enforce descriptor-only rows and the authority boundary: validateSettlementCoreCp451P09TestHermesSliceDescriptor (validators.js:4601-4608) rejects any promotion of Claude to final approval, any enterprise-trust-from-local-validation claim, and any claude_is_final_approval drift. The contract projection (contracts/settlement-core-contract.json p09_test_hermes_slice_descriptor) is deep-equal verified against the code-generated descriptor by both the validator script (1419-1423) and model.test.js, so there is no code/contract drift. Hermes is used solely as a deterministic review packet/receipt descriptor (H14 evidence, receipt_shape descriptor_only...no_write; emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product false) and not for product runtime. Claude remains a read-only reviewer (allowed_tools Read/Grep/Glob; read_only true) and not the final approver; human approval and the enterprise trust claim are handled outside this receipt. The pack honestly declares production_ready:false / status implemented_pending_review (command-evidence and manifest agree), exposing only a production_ready_candidate from the Hermes packet, which is the correct deferred posture. CP178+ hardening is satisfied: the inherited forbidden_review_evidence list covers auth failures, zero-byte stdout, malformed JSON, fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, and final-approval/enterprise-trust claims; the readiness auth-preflight passed with valid (non-empty, well-formed, unfenced) JSON and exit 0. The post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) correctly do not yet exist for CP00-451 because they are produced downstream of this review (steps 4-7 of the hardening sequence); the completed CP00-450 directory contains them. No P0/P1/P2 issues found. One non-blocking P3 cosmetic note on the generated changed-file-scope artifact. Note: this verdict is independent-reviewer advisory only and does not constitute final approval.","findings":[{"id":"CP00-451-P3-001","severity":"P3","title":"changed-file-scope.json double-lists the pack's own evidence directory under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved","file":"artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-451/changed-file-scope.json","line":138,"details":"docs/closeout-packs/cp00-451/ (and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md) appear in both untracked_implementation_files (lines 131-132) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 137-138). The cp00-451/ directory is part of CP00-451: it holds manifest.json and command-evidence.json, is referenced in required_pack_artifacts, and the commit_policy commits it under message 'Close CP00-451 settlement core p09 test hermes slice descriptor pack'. The manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest.json:1719-1727) correctly EXCLUDES this directory, so the discrepancy is confined to the generated scope artifact and reflects a 'preserved-in-worktree' vs 'unrelated-to-pack' naming conflation. Importantly, active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty, so no required file is dropped from scope and pack integrity is unaffected.","recommendation":"Optionally refine the changed-file-scope generator so a pack's own evidence directory is not also emitted under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (or rename the field to reflect 'preserved in worktree').
